summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the incomplete prototype pollution protection in set-in can be bypassed using Object.prototype method names, allowing an attacker to pollute Function.prototype and affect all functions globally.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that setIn({}, ['toString', 'call', 'evilProp'], 'EVIL_PAYLOAD') successfully pollutes Function.prototype.call.evilProp with the attacker-controlled payload, affecting all JavaScript functions.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the target repository, installed dependencies, executed the exploit script, and confirmed that Function.prototype.call.evilProp was set to 'EVIL_PAYLOAD'. The exploit demonstrated global impact by showing innocentFunction.call.evilProp and Array.prototype.map.call.evilProp also contained the payload.
verification_method: The exploit script checks that Function.prototype.call.evilProp === 'EVIL_PAYLOAD' after calling setIn with the malicious path, and verifies that innocentFunction.call.evilProp and Array.prototype.map.call.evilProp also contain the payload, proving global impact.
evidence_produced_by_run: Function.prototype.call.evilProp was successfully set to 'EVIL_PAYLOAD' through the bypass
The pollution affected all functions: innocentFunction.call.evilProp and Array.prototype.map.call.evilProp both contained the payload
The incomplete POLLUTED_KEYS blocklist (only __proto__, constructor, prototype) failed to prevent Object.prototype method names from being used in the attack path

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out version 2.0.3, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
